convention: anthropics/skills/skill-creator evals.json — trigger-only fixture per references/schemas.md. Each entry carries `should_trigger` so skill-creator's description-optimisation loop can score train/held-out trigger accuracy.
notes: Positives target port / second-implementation / conformance prompts for the eight in-scope JS-cross-compile-to-React+VDOM hosts (CLJS, TS, Melange/ReScript/Reason, Fable, Squint, Scala.js, PureScript, Kotlin/JS). Negatives target app authoring on the CLJS reference, live-runtime work, v1 migration (sibling skills), and out-of-scope hosts (non-React substrates / non-cross-compile-to-JS languages — out of scope per spec/000-Vision.md scope footnote).

evals.name: port-typescript
evals.prompt: I want to port re-frame2 to TypeScript. Where do I start?

evals.name: port-fable
evals.prompt: Build me a second re-frame2 implementation in F# using Fable. What decisions do I need to make first?

evals.name: port-squint
evals.prompt: Can I implement re-frame2 in Squint for a browser app? What does the identity primitive look like there?

evals.name: port-kotlin-js
evals.prompt: I'm thinking of porting re-frame2 to Kotlin/JS with kotlin-wrappers React. What's the implementor checklist?

evals.name: conformance-suite
evals.prompt: How do I run the spec/conformance/ tests against my partial port?

evals.name: primitive-decisions
evals.prompt: My host language has structural records but no protocols — what re-frame2 primitives does that affect?

evals.name: phase-2-order
evals.prompt: What EP order should I implement re-frame2 in? Frames first, or the registrar?

evals.name: scope-substrate
evals.prompt: If I'm writing a new Helix React-binding adapter on the CLJS impl, is that a port or just a new adapter?

evals.name: neg-author-app
evals.prompt: Write a re-frame2 reg-event-fx that loads articles from /api/articles.
evals.rationale: Authoring code on the CLJS reference is re-frame2's job.

evals.name: neg-setup
evals.prompt: Start me a new re-frame2 ClojureScript project from scratch.

evals.name: neg-live-pair
evals.prompt: Attach to my running shadow-cljs build and tell me what's in app-db.

evals.name: neg-migration
evals.prompt: I want to migrate my re-frame v1 app to re-frame2. Walk me through it.

evals.name: neg-spec-discussion
evals.prompt: Explain the EP rationale for why re-frame2 separates effects from coeffects.

evals.name: neg-causa-tour
evals.prompt: Which Causa panel shows machine state?
evals.rationale: Causa tour is its own skill.

evals.name: neg-vocab-port
evals.prompt: I'm porting Redux from JavaScript to TypeScript — any tips on the action-creator pattern?
evals.rationale: Vocabulary hit on 'port' but unrelated framework.

evals.name: neg-improver
evals.prompt: Review my re-frame2 source for anti-patterns.
evals.rationale: Critique mode on CLJS code is re-frame2-improver.

evals.name: neg-out-of-scope-python
evals.prompt: Can I implement re-frame2 in Python for a terminal UI app?
evals.rationale: Out of scope: Python is not a cross-compile-to-JS host and a terminal UI is not React+VDOM. Per spec/000-Vision.md scope footnote, only the eight in-scope JS-React hosts are implementation targets.

evals.name: neg-out-of-scope-rust
evals.prompt: I'm thinking of porting re-frame2 to Rust for an egui frontend. What's the implementor checklist?
evals.rationale: Out of scope: native Rust is not a cross-compile-to-JS host and egui is not React+VDOM. Deliberate scope choice, not an oversight (spec/000-Vision.md).

evals.name: neg-out-of-scope-substrate
evals.prompt: How would I build re-frame2 against a Vue or Svelte substrate instead of React?
evals.rationale: Out of scope: re-frame2 commits to React+VDOM at the render side (spec/006-ReactiveSubstrate.md); non-React substrates are out of scope.
